rag_rules: Attach each generated annex block to the declaration whose scope exposes every referenced symbol.

Keep implementation-owned annex content in implementation scope after relevant structural elements; do not place implementation-only paths in a type-owned annex unless they are surfaced through valid features or helpers.
generic_rules: Preserve existing component, subcomponent, connection, and property declarations unchanged except for the annex insertion.
Match the annex owner to symbol visibility: feature-level references stay with the type owner, implementation-only references stay with the implementation owner.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Insert the annex as a self-contained block inside the chosen owner body without breaking surrounding AADL structure.
Maintain local ordering and formatting conventions, especially around subcomponents, connections, properties, and any existing annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not move, rename, or rewrite architectural elements just to accommodate an annex.
Do not fuse content into a scope where referenced names are not visible.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal recurrence and branching expression patterns inside clauses
Validator-driven type repair or property-name-as-variable diagnostics
